capabilities.name: Signal Engine
capabilities.description: Extracts latest hammer or shooting-star signals from candle data and enriches them with Fibonacci levels, invalidation, RSI, divergence, trigger metadata, and higher-timeframe bias.
capabilities.safety_boundary: Produces signal records only; does not place orders.

capabilities.name: Runtime Market Loop
capabilities.description: Reads BTCUSDT market data, resamples Hammer timeframes, captures candles, appends signals and outcomes, and opens/closes local paper positions when paper mode is enabled.
capabilities.safety_boundary: Uses paper execution adapter by default.

capabilities.name: Signal Candidate Gate
capabilities.description: Filters signals by enabled timeframe, minimum hammer strength, higher-timeframe bias alignment, and recent same-direction duplicate spacing.
capabilities.safety_boundary: Marks tradable candidate status for paper/operator workflows only.

capabilities.name: Paper Trading
capabilities.description: Creates and closes deterministic local paper positions and records paper executions without contacting an exchange.
capabilities.safety_boundary: No live order, no Binance order endpoint, no real balance dependency.

capabilities.name: Strategy Memory
capabilities.description: Stores signals, outcomes, paper positions, paper executions, manual outcomes, strategy performance summaries, promotion status, and source-chain evidence.
capabilities.safety_boundary: Memory can inform readiness but cannot bypass approval or execution gates.

capabilities.name: Risk Engine
capabilities.description: Validates tiny-live risk contracts, notional caps, leverage caps, max loss, margin meaning, lane identity, exchange minimum compatibility, and live-disabled fields.
capabilities.safety_boundary: Risk contract validity is prerequisite evidence, not execution permission.

capabilities.name: Lane System
capabilities.description: Classifies exact lane keys by configured mode, freshness windows, risk limits, live eligibility, and global gate state.
capabilities.safety_boundary: Lane controls express operator intent only and do not create order payloads.

capabilities.name: Approval Gates
capabilities.description: Records operator decisions, exact approval phrases, human confirmations, final approval intent, Telegram challenges, and review packets.
capabilities.safety_boundary: Approval records are review artifacts unless separate execution gates pass.

capabilities.name: Execution Gates
capabilities.description: Composes final preflight, activation gate, first-live execution gate, connector readiness, protective readiness, exact confirmation, and idempotency.
capabilities.safety_boundary: Default output remains blocked/no-order unless future explicit live gates authorize otherwise.

capabilities.name: Kill Switches
capabilities.description: Keeps global kill switch and lane-level kill-switch rehearsal visible as authoritative blockers for live paths.
capabilities.safety_boundary: Kill switch is active by default and must not be bypassed.

capabilities.name: Operator API and CLI
capabilities.description: Provides local operator endpoints and inspect commands for readiness, tickets, paper, live-safety, live-arming, Telegram, strategy performance, and lane status.
capabilities.safety_boundary: Operator surfaces must stay local/private and must not imply live approval.

capabilities.name: External Reality Adapters
capabilities.description: Reads market data and can report safe connector or Binance status fields while keeping secrets hidden.
capabilities.safety_boundary: Live order, account, and signed request behavior is default-blocked and must be explicitly authorized.
